title: Acokanthera pubescens

intro: Acokanthera pubescens, also known as the "white milk bush," is a small, deciduous shrub native to southern Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Acokanthera pubescens include cardiac glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acokanthera pubescens is often prepared as a tea or used in the form of a tincture or infusion, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
